author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 31–36.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what follows of the previous lesson, and has been read publicly to us to-day from the holy Gospel, I then deferred speaking, because I had already said much, and of that liberty into which the grace of the Saviour calleth us it was needful to treat in no cursory or negligent way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of this, by the Lord’s help, we purpose speaking to you to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those to whom the Lord Jesus Christ was speaking were Jews, in a large measure indeed His enemies, but also in some measure already become, and yet to be, His friends; for some He saw there, as we have already said, who should yet believe after His passion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Looking to these, He had said, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am [He].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There also were those who, when He so spake, straightway believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To them He spake what we have heard to-day: “Then said Jesus to those Jews who believed on Him, If ye continue in my word, ye shall be my disciples indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By continuing ye shall be so; for as now ye are believers, by so continuing ye shall be beholders.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence there follows, “And ye shall know the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The truth is unchangeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The truth is bread, which refreshes our minds and fails not; changes the eater, and is not itself changed into the eater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The truth itself is the Word of God, God with God, the only-begotten Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Truth was for our sake clothed with flesh, that He might be born of the Virgin Mary, and the prophecy fulfilled, “Truth has sprung from the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Truth then, when speaking to the Jews, lay hid in the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He lay hid not in order to be denied, but to be deferred [in His manifestation]; to be deferred, in order to suffer in the flesh; and to suffer in the flesh, in order that flesh might be redeemed from sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so our Lord Jesus Christ, standing full in sight as regards the infirmity of flesh, but hid as regards the majesty of Godhead, said to those who had believed on Him, when He so spake, “If ye continue in my word, ye shall be my disciples indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And ye shall know the truth,” which now is hid from you, and speaks to you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the truth shall free you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This word, liberabit [shall free], the Lord hath taken from libertas [freedom].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For liberat [frees, delivers] is properly nothing else but liberum facit [makes free].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As salvat [he saves] is nothing else but salvum facit [he makes safe]; as he heals is nothing else but he makes whole; he enriches is nothing else but he makes rich; so liberat [he frees] is nothing else but liberum facit [he makes free].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is clearer in the Greek word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in Latin usage we commonly say that a man is delivered (liberari), in regard not to liberty, but only to safety, just as one is said to be delivered from some infirmity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So is it said customarily, but not properly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord made such use of this word in saying, “And the truth shall make you free (liberabit),” that in the Greek tongue no one could doubt that He spake of freedom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, the Jews also so understood and “answered Him;” not those who had already believed, but those in that crowd who were not yet believers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They answered Him, We are Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be free?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord had not said, “Ye shall be free,” but, “The truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That word, however, they, because, as I have said, it is clearly so in the Greek, understood as pointing only to freedom, and puffed themselves up as Abraham’s seed, and said, “We are Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be free?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O inflated skin! such is not magnanimity, but windy swelling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even as regards freedom in this life, how was that the truth when you said, “We were never in bondage to any man”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not Joseph sold?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were not the holy prophets led into captivity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And again, did not that very nation, when making bricks in Egypt, also serve hard rulers, not only in gold and silver, but also in clay?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you were never in bondage to any man, ungrateful people, why is it that God is continually reminding you that He delivered you from the house of bondage?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or mean you, perchance, that your fathers were in bondage, but you who speak were never in bondage to any man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then were you now paying tribute to the Romans, out of which also you formed a trap for the Truth Himself, as if to ensnare Him, when you said, “Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar?” in order that, had He said, It is lawful, you might fasten on Him as one ill-disposed to the liberty of Abraham’s seed; and if He said, It is not lawful, you might slander Him before the kings of the earth, as forbidding the payment of tribute to such?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Deservedly were you defeated on producing the money, and compelled yourselves to concur in your own capture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there it was told you, “Render to Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and to God the things that are God’s,” after your own reply, that the money-piece bore the image of Cæsar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as Cæsar looks for his own image on the coin, so God looks for His in man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, then, did He answer the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am moved, brethren, by the hollow pride of men, because even of that very freedom of theirs, which they understood carnally, they lied when they said, “We were never in bondage to any man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to the Lord’s own answer, let us give better and more earnest heed, lest we ourselves be also found bondmen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that every one who committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is the servant—would that it were of man, and not of sin!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will not tremble at such words?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord our God grant us, that is, both you and me, that I may speak in fitting terms of this freedom to be sought, and of that bondage to be avoided.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Amen, amen [verily, verily], I say unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Truth speaks: and in what sense does the Lord our God claim it as His to say, “Amen, amen, I say unto you”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His charge is weighty in so announcing it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In some sort, if lawful to be said, His form of swearing is, “Amen, amen, I say unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Amen in a way may be interpreted, [It is] true [truly, verily]; and yet it is not interpreted, though it might have been said, What is true [verily] I say unto you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither the Greek translator nor the Latin has dared to do so; for this word Amen is neither Greek nor Latin, but Hebrew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So it has remained without interpretation, to possess honor as the covering of something hidden; not in order to be disowned, but that it might not, as a thing laid bare to the eye, fall into disrepute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet it is not once, but twice uttered by the Lord, “Amen, amen, I say unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now learn from the very doubling, how much was implied in the charge before us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is the charge given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, saith the Truth who surely, though He had not said, Verily, I say, could not possibly lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet [thereby] He impresses, inculcates His charge, arouses in a way the sleeping, makes them attentive, and would not be contemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, that every one who committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Miserable slavery!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men frequently, when they suffer under wicked masters, demand to get themselves sold, not seeking to be without a master, but at all events to change him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can the servant of sin do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom can he make his demand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom apply for redress?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of whom require himself to be sold?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then at times a man’s slave, worn out by the commands of an unfeeling master, finds rest in flight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither can the servant of sin flee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Himself he carries with him wherever he flees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: An evil conscience flees not from itself; it has no place to go to; it follows itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, he cannot withdraw from himself, for the sin he commits is within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has committed sin to obtain some bodily pleasure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The pleasure passes away; the sin remains.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What delighted is gone; the sting has remained behind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Evil bondage!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes men flee to the Church, and we generally permit them, uninstructed as they are—men, wishing to be rid of their master, who are unwilling to be rid of their sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But sometimes also those subjected to an unlawful and wicked yoke flee for refuge to the Church; for, though free-born men, they are retained in bondage: and an appeal is made to the bishop.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And unless he care to put forth every effort to save free-birth from oppression, he is accounted unmerciful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us all flee to Christ, and appeal against sin to God as our deliverer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us seek to get ourselves sold, that we may be redeemed by His blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord says, “Ye were sold for nought, and ye shall be redeemed without money.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Without price, that is, of your own; because of mine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So saith the Lord; for He Himself has paid the price, not in money, but His own blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Otherwise we had remained both bondmen and indigent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this bondage, then, we are set free by the Lord alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who had it not, Himself delivers us from it; for He alone came without sin in the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the little ones whom you see carried in their mothers’ hands cannot yet walk, and are already in fetters; for they have received from Adam what they are loosened from by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To them also, when baptized, pertains that grace which is promised by the Lord; for He only can deliver from sin who came without sin, and was made a sacrifice for sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For you heard when the apostle was read: “We are ambassadors,” he says, “for Christ, as though God were exhorting you by us; we beseech you in Christ’s stead,”—that is, as if Christ were beseeching you, and for what?—“to be reconciled unto God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the apostle exhorts and beseeches us to be reconciled unto God, then were we enemies to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no one is reconciled unless from a state of enmity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we have become enemies not by nature, but by sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the same source are we the servants of sin, that we are the enemies of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God has no enemies in a state of freedom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They must be slaves; and slaves will they remain unless delivered by Him to whom they wished by their sins to be enemies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, says be, “We beseech you in Christ’s stead to be reconciled unto God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how are we reconciled, save by the removal of that which separates between us and Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He says by the prophet, “He hath not made the ear heavy that it should not hear; but your iniquities have separated between you and your God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, then, we are not reconciled, unless that which is in the midst is taken away, and something else is put in its place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is a separating medium, and, on the other hand, there is a reconciling Mediator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The separating medium is sin, the reconciling Mediator is the Lord Jesus Christ: “For there is one God and Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To take then away the separating wall, which is sin, that Mediator has come, and the priest has Himself become the sacrifice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because He was made a sacrifice for sin, offering Himself as a whole burnt-offering on the cross of His passion, the apostle, after saying, “We beseech you in Christ’s stead to be reconciled unto God,”—as if we had said, How shall we be able to be reconciled?—goes on to say, “He hath made Him,” that is, Christ Himself, “who knew no sin, [to be] sin for us, that we may be the righteousness of God in Him,” “Him,” he says, Christ Himself our God, “who knew no sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He came in the flesh, that is, in the likeness of sinful flesh, but not in sinful flesh, because He had no sin at all; and therefore became a true sacrifice for sin, because He Himself had no sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps, through some special perception of my own, I have said that sin is a sacrifice for sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let those who have read it be free to acknowledge it; let not those who have not read it be backward; let them not, I say, be backward to read, that they may be truthful in judging.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when God gave commandment about the offering of sacrifices for sin, in which sacrifices there was no expiation of sins, but the shadow of things to come, the self-same sacrifices, the self-same offerings, the self-same victims, the self-same animals, which were brought forward to be slain for sins, and in whose blood that [true] blood was prefigured, are themselves called sinsby the law; and that to such an extent that in certain passages it is written in these terms, that the priests, when about to sacrifice, were to lay their hands on the head of the sin, that is, on the head of the victim about to be sacrificed for sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such sin, then, that is, such a sacrifice for sin, was our Lord Jesus Christ made, “who knew no sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With efficacious merit does He deliver from this bondage of sin, who saith in the psalms: “I am become as a man without help, free among the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He only was free, because He had no sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He Himself says in the Gospel, “Behold, the prince of this world cometh,” meaning the devil about to come in the persons of the persecuting Jews;—“behold,” He says, “he cometh, and shall find nothing in me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not as he found some measure of sin in those whom he also slew as righteous; in me he shall find nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just as if He were asked, If he shall find nothing in Thee, wherefore will he slay Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He further said, “But that all may know that I do the will of my Father, rise and let us go hence.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not, He says, pay the penalty of death as a necessity of my sinfulness; but in the death I die, I do the will of my Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in this, I am doing rather than enduring it; for, were I unwilling, I should not have had the suffering to endure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have Him saying in another place, “I have power to lay down my life, and I have power to take it up again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here surely is one “free among the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, every one that committeth sin is the servant of sin, listen to what is our hope of liberty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the servant,” He says, “abideth not in the house for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The church is the house, the servant is the sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many sinners enter the church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly He has not said, “The servant” is not in the house, but “abideth not in the house for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, there shall be no servant there, who will be there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “when” as the Scripture speaketh, “the righteous king sitteth on the throne, who will boast of having a clean heart? or who will boast that he is pure from his sin?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has greatly alarmed us, my brethren, by saying, “The servant abideth not in the house for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He further adds, “But the Son abideth ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will Christ, then, be alone in His house?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will no people remain at His side?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose head will He be, if there shall be no body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is the Son all this, both the head and the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not without cause that He has inspired both terror and hope: terror, in order that we should not love sin; and hope, that we should not be distrustful of the remission of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Every one,” He says, “that committeth sin is the servant of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the servant abideth not in the house for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What hope, then, have we, who are not without sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to thy hope: “The Son abideth for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Son, therefore, shall make you free, then shall ye be free indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our hope is this, brethren, to be made free by the free One; and that, in setting us free, He may make us His servants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we were the servants of lust; but being set free, we are made the servants of love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This also the apostle says: “For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not then the Christian say, I am free; I have been called unto liberty: I was a slave, but have been redeemed, and by my very redemption have been made free, I shall do what I please: no one may balk me of my will, if I am free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou committest sin with such a will, thou art the servant of sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not then abuse your liberty for freedom in sinning, but use it for the purpose of sinning not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For only if thy will is pious, will it be free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt be free, if thou art a servant still,—free from sin, the servant of righteous ness: as the apostle says, “When ye were the servants of sin, ye were free from righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, being made free from sin, and become servants to God, ye have your fruit unto holiness, and the end everlasting life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us be striving after the latter, and be doing the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The first stage of liberty is to be free from crimes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, my brethren, give heed, that I may not by any means mislead your understanding as to the nature of that liberty at present, and what it will be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sift any one soever of the highest integrity in this life, and however worthy he may already be of the name of upright, yet is he not without sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to Saint John himself, the author of the Gospel before us, when he says in his epistle, “If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He alone could say this who was “free among the dead:” of Him only could it be said, who knew no sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It could be said only of Him, for He also “was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He alone could say, “Behold, the prince of this world cometh, and shall find nothing in me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sift any one else, who is accounted righteous, yet is he not in all respects without sin; not even such as was Job, to whom the Lord bore such testimony, that the devil was filled with envy, and demanded that he should be tempted, and was himself defeated in the temptation, to the end that Job might be proved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he was proved for this reason, not that the certainty of his carrying off the conqueror’s wreath was unknown to God, but that he might become known as an object of imitation to others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what says Job himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For who is clean? not even the infant whose life is but a day’s span upon the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is plain that many are called righteous without opposition, because the term is understood as meaning, free from crime; for in human affairs there is no just ground of complaint attaching to those who are free from criminal conduct.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But crime is grievous sin, deserving in the highest measure to be denounced and condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, however, that God condemns certain sins, and justifies and praises certain others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He approves of none.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He hates them all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the physician dislikes the ailment of the ailing, and works by his healing measures to get the ailment removed and the ailing relieved; so God by his grace worketh in us, that sin may be consumed, and man made free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when, you will be saying, is it consumed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it is lessened, why is it not consumed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is growing less in the life of those who are advancing onwards, which is consumed in the life of those who have attained to perfection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The first stage of liberty, then, is to be free from crimes [sinful conduct].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so the Apostle Paul, when he determined on the ordination of either elders or deacons, or whoever was to be ordained to the superintendence of the Church, says not, If any one is without sin; for had he said so, every one would be rejected as unfit, none would be ordained: but he says, “If any one is without crime” [E.V. blame], such as, murder, adultery, any uncleanness of fornication, theft, fraud, sacrilege, and others of that sort.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When a man has begun to be free from these (and every Christian man ought to be so), he begins to raise his head to liberty; but that is liberty begun, not completed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, says some one, is it not completed liberty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, “I see another law in my members warring against the law of my mind;” “for what I would,” he says, “that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The flesh,” he says, “lusteth against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh; so that ye do not the things that ye would.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In part liberty, in part bondage: not yet entire, not yet pure, not yet full liberty, because not yet eternity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have still infirmity in part, in part we have attained to liberty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever has been our sin, was previously wiped out in baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because all our iniquity has been blotted out, has there remained no infirmity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If there had not, we should be living here without sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet who would venture to say so, but the proud, but the man unworthy of the Deliverer’s mercy, but he who wishes to be self-deceived, and who is destitute of the truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, from the fact that some infirmity remains, I venture to say that, in what measure we serve God, we are free; in what measure we serve the law of sin, we are still in bondage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence says the apostle, what we began to say, “I delight in the law of God after the inward man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here then it is, wherein we are free, wherein we delight in the law of God; for liberty has joy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as long as it is from fear that thou doest what is right, God is no delight to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Find thy delight in Him, and thou art free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fear not punishment, but love righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou not yet able to love righteousness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fear even punishment, that thou mayest attain to the love of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the measure then spoken of above, he felt himself to be already free, and there fore said, “I delight in the law of God after the inward man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I delight in the law, I delight in its requirements, I delight in righteousness itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But I see another law in my members”—this infirmity which remains—“warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin, which is in my members.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this side he feels his captivity, where righteousness has not been perfected; for where he delights in the law of God, he is not the captive but the friend of the law; and therefore free, because a friend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is to be done with that which so remains?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but to look to Him who has said, “If the Son shall make you free, then shall ye be free indeed”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Indeed he also who thus spake so looked to Him: “O wretched man that I am,” he says, “who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I thank God, through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “if the Son shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then he concluded thus: “So then, with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I myself, he says; for there are not two of us contrary to each other, coming from different origins; but “with the mind I myself serve the law of God, and with the flesh the law of sin,” so long as languor struggles against salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if with the flesh thou servest the law of sin, do as the apostle himself says: “Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lust thereof: neither yield ye your members as weapons of unrighteousness unto sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says not, Let it not be; but, “Let it not reign.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So long as sin must be in thy members, let its reigning power at least be taken away, let not its demands be obeyed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does anger rise?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yield not up thy tongue to anger for the purpose of evil-speaking; yield not up thy hand or foot to anger for the purpose of striking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That irrational anger would not rise, were there no sin in the members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But take away its ruling power; let it have no weapons wherewith to fight against thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then also it will learn not to rise, when it begins to find the lack of weapons.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Yield not your members as weapons of unrighteousness unto sin,” else will ye be entirely captive, and there will be no room to say, “With the mind I serve the law of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the mind keep possession of the weapons, the members are not roused to the service of raging sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the inward ruler keep possession of the citadel, because it stands there under a greater ruler, and is certain of assistance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it bridle anger; let it restrain evil desire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is within something that needs bridling, that needs restraining, that needs to be kept in command.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what did that righteous man wish, who with the mind was serving the law of God, but that there should be a complete deliverance from that which needed to be bridled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this ought every one to be striving after who is aiming at perfection, that lust itself also, no longer receiving the obedience of the members, may every day be lessened in the advancing pilgrim.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To will,” he says, “is present with me; but not so, how to perfect that which is good.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has he said, To do good is not present with me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had he said so, hope would be wanting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not say, To do is not present with me, but, “To perfect is not present with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is the perfecting of good, but the elimination and end of evil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is the elimination of evil, but what the law says, “Thou shalt not lust [covet]”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To lust not at all is the perfecting of good, because it is the eliminating of evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This he said, “To perfect that which is good is not present with me,” because his doing could not get the length of setting him free from lust.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He labored only to bridle lust, to refuse consent to lust, and not to yield his members to its service.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To perfect,” then, he says, “that which is good is not present with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I cannot fulfill the commandment, “Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is needed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To fulfill this: “Go not after thy lusts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do this meanwhile so long as unlawful lusts are present in thy flesh; “Go not after thy lusts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abide in the service of God, in the liberty of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With the mind serve the law of thy God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yield not thyself to thy lusts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By following them, thou addest to their strength.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By giving them strength, how canst thou conquer, when on thine own strength thou art nourishing enemies against thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is that full and perfect liberty in the Lord Jesus, who said, “If the Son shall make you free, then shall ye be free indeed;” and when shall it be a full and perfect liberty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When enmities are no more; when “death, the last enemy, shall be destroyed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.—And when this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O death, where is thy struggle?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, “O death, where is thy struggle”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The flesh lusteth against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh,” but only when the flesh of sin was in vigor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “O death, where is [now] thy struggle?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now shall we live, no more shall we die, in Him who died for us and rose again: “that they,” he says, “who live, should no longer live unto themselves, but unto Him who died for them and rose again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us be praying, as those who are wounded, for the physician; let us be carried into the inn to be healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is He who promises salvation, who pitied the man left half-alive on the road by robbers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He poured in oil and wine, He healed the wounds, He put him on his beast, He took him to the inn, He commended him to the innkeeper’s care.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what innkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps to him who said, “We are ambassadors for Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gave also two pence to pay for the healing of the wounded man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And perhaps these are the two commandments, on which hang all the law and the prophets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, is the Church also, wherein the wounded is healed meanwhile, the traveller’s inn; but above the Church itself, lies the possessor’s inheritance.
